console.description: The Console object provides access to the browser's debugging console (e.g. the Web console in Firefox). Implementations available in Node.js, Deno, and browsers.

log.description: Outputs a message to the console.

error.description: Outputs an error message. May include stack trace.

table.description: Displays tabular data as a table.

dir.description: Displays an interactive listing of properties of a specified object.

time.description: Starts a timer you can use to track how long an operation takes.

timeEnd.description: Stops a timer and logs elapsed time.

timeLog.description: Logs the current value of a timer started by console.time().

assert.description: Log an error message if the assertion is false.

count.description: Log the number of times this line has been called with the given label.

countReset.description: Resets the value of the counter with the given label.

group.description: Creates a new inline group, indenting all following output.

groupCollapsed.description: Creates a new inline group (collapsed by default).

JSON.description: An intrinsic object that provides functions to convert JavaScript values to and from JSON format.

parse.description: Converts a JSON string into an object.

parameters.name: reviver
parameters.description: Optional function that transforms the results

stringify.signature: JSON.stringify(value: any, replacer?: Function | (string|number)[], space?: string | number): string
stringify.description: Converts a JavaScript value to a JSON string.

Array.description: The Array object enables storing a collection of multiple items under a single variable name. Provides numerous instance methods for traversing and mutation.

from.signature: Array.from<T, U>(arrayLike: Iterable<T> | ArrayLike<T>, mapFn?: (v: T, k: number) => U): U[]
from.description: Creates a new, shallow-copied Array from an iterable or array-like object.

isArray.description: Determines whether the passed value is an Array.

of.description: Creates a new Array instance from a variable number of arguments.

fromAsync.description: ES2024 — Creates a new Array from an async iterable.

map.signature: Array<T>.map<U>(callbackfn: (value: T, index: number, array: T[]) => U): U[]
map.description: Creates a new array with the results of calling a function on every element.

filter.signature: Array<T>.filter(predicate: (value: T, index: number) => boolean): T[]
filter.description: Creates a new array with all elements that pass the test.

reduce.signature: Array<T>.reduce<U>(callbackfn: (acc: U, cur: T, idx: number) => U, initialValue: U): U
reduce.description: Reduces the array to a single value by running a reducer on each element.

forEach.signature: Array<T>.forEach(callbackfn: (value: T, index: number) => void): void
forEach.description: Executes a provided function once for each array element.

find.signature: Array<T>.find(predicate: (value: T, index: number) => boolean): T | undefined
find.description: Returns the first element satisfying the testing function.

findIndex.signature: Array<T>.findIndex(predicate: (value: T, index: number) => boolean): number
findIndex.description: Returns the index of the first element satisfying the testing function, or -1.

findLast.signature: Array<T>.findLast(predicate: (value: T, index: number) => boolean): T | undefined
findLast.description: Returns the last element satisfying the testing function.

findLastIndex.signature: Array<T>.findLastIndex(predicate: (value: T, index: number) => boolean): number
findLastIndex.description: Returns the index of the last element satisfying the testing function, or -1.

some.signature: Array<T>.some(predicate: (value: T, index: number) => boolean): boolean
some.description: Tests whether at least one element passes the test.

every.signature: Array<T>.every(predicate: (value: T, index: number) => boolean): boolean
every.description: Tests whether all elements pass the test.

includes.description: Determines whether an array includes a certain value.

indexOf.description: Returns the first index at which a given element can be found, or -1.

flat.description: Creates a new array with all sub-array elements concatenated recursively up to depth.

flatMap.signature: Array<T>.flatMap<U>(callback: (value: T, index: number) => U | U[]): U[]
flatMap.description: Maps each element then flattens the result (depth 1).

sort.signature: Array<T>.sort(compareFn?: (a: T, b: T) => number): T[]

splice.signature: Array<T>.splice(start: number, deleteCount?: number, ...items: T[]): T[]
splice.description: Changes contents by removing or replacing existing elements.

slice.description: Returns a shallow copy of a portion of an array.

concat.description: Merges two or more arrays into a new array.

join.description: Joins all elements into a string with separator.

push.description: Adds elements to the end and returns the new length.

pop.description: Removes the last element and returns it.

shift.description: Removes the first element and returns it.

unshift.description: Adds elements to the beginning and returns the new length.

at.description: Returns the element at index, accepting negative integers.

fill.signature: Array<T>.fill(value: T, start?: number, end?: number): T[]
fill.description: Fills all positions from start to end with a static value.

entries.description: Returns a new array iterator with key/value pairs.

keys.description: Returns a new array iterator with the keys (indices).

values.description: Returns a new array iterator with the values.

toSorted.signature: Array<T>.toSorted(compareFn?: (a: T, b: T) => number): T[]
toSorted.description: ES2023 — Returns a new sorted array without modifying the original.

toReversed.description: ES2023 — Returns a new reversed array without modifying the original.

toSpliced.signature: Array<T>.toSpliced(start: number, deleteCount?: number, ...items: T[]): T[]
toSpliced.description: ES2023 — Returns a new array with some elements removed/replaced, without modifying the original.

with.description: ES2023 — Returns a new array with the element at index replaced.

Object.description: The Object type represents one of JavaScript's data types. It is used to store various keyed collections and more complex entities.

assign.description: Copies enumerable own properties from sources to target.

fromEntries.description: Transforms key-value pairs into an object.

create.signature: Object.create(proto: object | null, propertiesObject?: object): any
create.description: Creates a new object with the specified prototype.

defineProperty.signature: Object.defineProperty(obj: object, prop: string | symbol, descriptor: PropertyDescriptor): object
defineProperty.description: Defines a new property directly on an object.

getOwnPropertyNames.description: Returns all own property names (including non-enumerable).

getPrototypeOf.description: Returns the prototype of the specified object.

hasOwn.signature: Object.hasOwn(obj: object, prop: string | symbol): boolean
hasOwn.description: ES2022 — Returns true if the object has the specified own property.

groupBy.signature: Object.groupBy<T>(items: Iterable<T>, callbackFn: (el: T, idx: number) => string): Record<string, T[]>
groupBy.description: ES2024 — Groups elements by a callback's return value.

Promise.description: Represents the eventual completion or failure of an asynchronous operation and its resulting value.

all.description: Resolves when all promises resolve; rejects when any rejects.

allSettled.description: Resolves when all promises settle (fulfilled or rejected).

race.description: Settles as soon as any promise settles.

any.description: Resolves with the first fulfilling promise. Rejects with AggregateError if all reject.

resolve.description: Returns a Promise resolved with the given value.

reject.description: Returns a Promise rejected with the given reason.

withResolvers.signature: Promise.withResolvers<T>(): { promise: Promise<T>, resolve: (v: T) => void, reject: (r: any) => void }
withResolvers.description: ES2024 — Returns a promise with its resolve and reject functions.

then.signature: Promise<T>.then<U>(onfulfilled?: (value: T) => U, onrejected?: (reason: any) => U): Promise<U>
then.description: Attaches callbacks for the resolution and/or rejection of the Promise.

catch.description: Attaches a callback for only the rejection of the Promise.

finally.description: Attaches a callback invoked when the Promise is settled (fulfilled or rejected).

Map.description: Holds key-value pairs and remembers the original insertion order. Any value may be used as key or value.

set.description: Sets the value for the key. Returns the Map object.

get.description: Returns the value associated with the key, or undefined.

has.description: Returns whether a value is associated with the key.

delete.description: Removes the entry for the key. Returns true if an entry was removed.

forEach.signature: Map<K,V>.forEach(callbackFn: (value: V, key: K, map: Map<K,V>) => void): void
forEach.description: Calls callbackFn once for each key-value pair.

entries.description: Returns an iterator of [key, value] pairs.

Set.description: Stores unique values of any type.

delete.description: Removes the value. Returns true if removed.

forEach.signature: Set<T>.forEach(callbackFn: (value: T, value2: T, set: Set<T>) => void): void
forEach.description: Calls callbackFn once for each value.

entries.description: Returns an iterator of [value, value] pairs.

union.description: ES2025 — Returns a new set with elements in either this or other.

intersection.description: ES2025 — Returns a new set with elements in both this and other.

difference.description: ES2025 — Returns a new set with elements in this but not in other.

isSubsetOf.description: ES2025 — Returns whether this is a subset of other.

String.description: The String object is used to represent and manipulate a sequence of characters.

charAt.description: Returns the character at the specified index.

charCodeAt.description: Returns the UTF-16 code unit at the specified index.

codePointAt.description: Returns the Unicode code point at the specified position.

includes.description: Determines whether the string contains the search string.

startsWith.description: Determines whether the string begins with the search string.

endsWith.description: Determines whether the string ends with the search string.

indexOf.description: Returns the index of the first occurrence, or -1.

lastIndexOf.description: Returns the index of the last occurrence, or -1.

match.description: Retrieves the result of matching against a regular expression.

matchAll.description: Returns an iterator of all results matching a regex (must have 'g' flag).

replace.signature: String.replace(searchValue: string | RegExp, replaceValue: string | Function): string
replace.description: Returns a new string with one or all matches replaced.

replaceAll.signature: String.replaceAll(searchValue: string | RegExp, replaceValue: string | Function): string
replaceAll.description: Returns a new string with all matches replaced.

search.description: Searches for a match and returns the index of the match.

slice.description: Extracts a section of a string and returns it as a new string.

split.signature: String.split(separator: string | RegExp, limit?: number): string[]
split.description: Divides a string into an ordered list of substrings.

substring.description: Returns the part of the string between the start and end indexes.

toLowerCase.description: Returns the calling string value converted to lowercase.

toUpperCase.description: Returns the calling string value converted to uppercase.

padStart.description: Pads the current string from the start.

padEnd.description: Pads the current string from the end.

repeat.description: Returns a string consisting of count copies of the string.

at.description: Returns the character at the given index, supporting negative indexing.

Math.description: A built-in object that has properties and methods for mathematical constants and functions.

round.description: Returns the value of x rounded to the nearest integer.

trunc.description: Returns the integer part of x by removing any fractional digits.

max.description: Returns the largest of zero or more numbers.

min.description: Returns the smallest of zero or more numbers.

random.description: Returns a pseudo-random number between 0 (inclusive) and 1 (exclusive).

pow.description: Returns base raised to the exponent power.

sqrt.description: Returns the square root of x.

cbrt.description: Returns the cube root of x.

log.description: Returns the natural logarithm (ln) of x.

log2.description: Returns the base-2 logarithm of x.

log10.description: Returns the base-10 logarithm of x.

sign.description: Returns the sign of x: -1, 0, or 1.

PI.description: The ratio of a circle's circumference to its diameter (~3.14159).

fetch.signature: function fetch(input: RequestInfo | URL, init?: RequestInit): Promise<Response>
fetch.description: Starts the process of fetching a resource from the network, returning a promise that resolves with the Response.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: The resource to fetch (URL string, URL object, or Request object)

parameters.name: init
parameters.description: Optional settings: method, headers, body, signal, mode, credentials, cache, redirect

setTimeout.signature: function setTimeout(callback: Function, delay?: number, ...args: any[]): number
setTimeout.description: Sets a timer which executes a function once the timer expires.

setInterval.signature: function setInterval(callback: Function, delay?: number, ...args: any[]): number
setInterval.description: Repeatedly calls a function with a fixed time delay between each call.

structuredClone.signature: function structuredClone<T>(value: T, options?: { transfer: Transferable[] }): T
structuredClone.description: Creates a deep clone of a given value using the structured clone algorithm.
